description: Resolve correctness, security, accessibility, packaging, and responsive-design findings from the repository audit.

userStories.title: Secure the SSR HTML renderer
userStories.description: As a package consumer, I want server-rendered code blocks to safely handle user-provided options so that content cannot inject markup or attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: Escape copyHint text before inserting it into generated HTML
Escape classes and attribute values before inserting them into generated HTML
Add regression tests covering quote, angle-bracket, and event-handler payloads
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix minified CommonJS package exports
userStories.description: As a CommonJS consumer, I want every advertised package export to exist so that requiring minified entry points does not fail at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Either build the advertised dist/*.min.cjs files or remove the invalid require mapping
Add a package export smoke check for the minified entry points
The package build completes without missing advertised files
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align SSR and hydrated copy-button accessibility
userStories.description: As a screen-reader user, I want the copy button to expose the same accessible name before and after hydration so that SSR does not produce contradictory announcements.
acceptanceCriteria: Use one shared default copy-button label for SSR and client hydration
Apply copyButtonLabel to the server-rendered button aria-label
Keep the live region empty until a copy status needs to be announced
Add tests for default and custom labels in SSR output and hydrated output
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preserve progressive enhancement without JavaScript
userStories.description: As a user with JavaScript disabled or delayed, I want code content to remain readable so that the component has a useful fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the rule that hides the entire code-block before custom-element definition
Plain-text and SSR-rendered code remain visible before JavaScript loads
Add a regression test or browser check for the pre-upgrade state
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make code blocks responsive and touch-friendly
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want long code and the copy control to remain usable within narrow viewports so that the page does not overflow or hide content.
acceptanceCriteria: Constrain code-block width to its containing area
Make overflowing pre content keyboard- and touch-scrollable
Reserve space so the copy control does not cover code
Give the copy control a minimum 24px target, preferably 44px
Verify narrow viewport behavior and keyboard focus in a browser
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate and document accessibility styling
userStories.description: As a package consumer, I want the documented CSS imports to produce a correctly styled accessible copy button without hidden implementation requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Include the visually-hidden utility and copy-button styles in the supported import path, or document all required imports
The default usage does not display the visually-hidden Copy text as visible content
Update README examples to match the supported import path
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make the demo page accessible and high-contrast
userStories.description: As a demo visitor using assistive technology or low vision, I want the example page and syntax theme to meet basic navigation and contrast expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a single descriptive h1 and a main landmark to the example page
Replace or adjust Prism token colors that fail 4.5:1 contrast against the code background
Define project-owned colors as CSS variables where the demo owns the styling
Verify the example at desktop and narrow viewports in a browser
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add audit checks to continuous integration
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want CI to catch the audited quality regressions so that packaging, CSS, and accessibility issues do not recur silently.
acceptanceCriteria: Run core stylelint checks in CI
Add a repeatable accessibility check for the demo or document the runtime limitation
Add a package-export smoke check to CI
CI uses reproducible dependency installation or documents why a lockfile is not used
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Apply the repository line-length convention
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want source lines kept below 80 columns so that the code remains readable in narrow terminals and reviews.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap source, test, and example lines that exceed 79 columns
Do not reformat vendored Prism output solely for this task
Add or configure a check that prevents new overlong project-owned lines
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
